prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, the wizards of "CloudSoft" hath conjured a revenue of $25 million in this quarter, a sum most wondrous and perplexing to mine own simple mind. Their "recurring subscription revenue" hath grown by 30% from the previous season, a rate of increase that doth rival the swiftness of a courser on a moonlit night. The chroniclers of "GAAP" hath decreed that the company's "operating expenses" shall be tallied at $18 million, a figure that seemeth to involve a multitude of mystifying calculations and whispered incantations. Forsooth, the leader of this company, a man named "CEO John," doth proclaim that their "customer acquisition costs" hath decreased by 25%, a marvel that I can scarce comprehend, save that it involve a series of intricate diagrams and hastily scribbled notes.
